projectDescription: Hiroshi is a local-first agentic AI developer assistant that runs on Ollama, supports multi-agent orchestration, custom skills, MCP servers, cron automation, and chat integrations with Telegram, Discord, and Slack.
keyFeatures: Interactive terminal agent mode with multi-agent orchestration via AGENTS.md
Background daemon mode with web dashboard at localhost:8080
Local Ollama integration for fully offline AI inference
Hybrid semantic memory with SQLite vector search and FTS5 (< 15ms lookups)
Custom skills system supporting Python, Bash, and other polyglot scripts
Native MCP (Model Context Protocol) client host for JSON-RPC tool servers
Cron scheduler for scheduled agent tasks
Proactive SOP engine for autonomous background routines
Chat gateways for Telegram, Discord, and Slack
Tailscale overlay network support for remote dashboard access
System diagnostics via hiroshi doctor command
Config migration from openclaw / zeroclaw platforms
publicApiSurface: hiroshi agent — interactive terminal chat mode
hiroshi daemon — boot background services (gateways, dashboard, SOP)
hiroshi service install|uninstall|start|stop|status — OS service management
hiroshi migrate --source <platform> — migrate from other agent platforms
~/.hiroshi/AGENTS.md — agent role definitions and handoff rules
SKILL.md frontmatter: name, description, schema fields
config.toml [ollama] section: host, model, temperature, context_window, embedding_model
config.toml [security] section: sandbox_path, allow_shell_commands, allowed_binaries, allowed_senders
config.toml [[cron.tasks]] section: name, schedule, agent, prompt
config.toml [sop] section: enabled, agent, interval_minutes, routines, notify_channels
